title: Registering an External Image File as a Private Image

reasons: The image is a plus sign icon, which visually represents the 'add' action described in the text.
It is explicitly referenced in the RST as |image1| to instruct users where to click to add data disks.
The icon aligns perfectly with the procedural step for configuring data disk parameters.

title: Creating a System Disk Image from a Linux ECS

reasons: The image is a flowchart diagram illustrating the process of creating a system disk image from an ECS and subsequently creating a new ECS from that image.
It directly corresponds to the document title and the 'Background' section which describes this exact workflow.
The diagram clearly shows the relationship between snapshots, ECS instances, and image creation steps.
No interactive UI elements are present, so color constraints for buttons do not apply.

title: Creating a System Disk Image from a Windows ECS

reasons: The image is a flowchart diagram that directly illustrates the four-step creation process outlined in the RST text.
It accurately depicts each step: preparing the image file, uploading to an OBS bucket, registering as a private image, and creating ECSs.
There are no contradictions between the visual diagram and the textual description.
